sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Cagliari are in strong form with 3 consecutive wins in their last 5 matches, scoring consistently. Inter have had a mixed run with 2 wins and 2 losses in their last 5, but they boast a potent attack led by Marcus Thuram with 5 goals recently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Inter have dominated recent encounters against Cagliari, winning 4 of the last 5 matchups, including a 3-0 away win and a 3-1 home victory in the last two head-to-head games. Cagliari have struggled to keep clean sheets against Inter, conceding in all 20 recent meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Cagliari have a solid defense, conceding only 3 goals this season (3rd best in the league), but their attack is moderate with 5 goals. Inter have scored 11 goals but conceded 7, showing defensive vulnerabilities despite a strong offensive output.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Inter's attacking prowess and historical dominance over Cagliari give them an edge, especially with key players like Hakan Çalhanoğlu in form. Cagliari's home form and defensive stability could make it competitive, but their low possession and final third entries might limit scoring chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between Cagliari and Inter at the Unipol Domus saw Inter take a commanding lead with a 1-0 scoreline. Inter dominated possession and created several scoring opportunities, culminating in an early headed goal by Lautaro Martínez in the 9th minute. Cagliari struggled to find their footing, failing to register a single shot on target or create any significant chances. Inter's control was evident with more entries into the final third and penalty area, while Cagliari's defensive efforts were notable but ultimately insufficient to prevent the deficit.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Cagliari
halftimeSections.text: Cagliari's performance in the first half was underwhelming, as they were unable to generate any meaningful attacking threat. Their possession was significantly lower compared to Inter, and they lacked creativity in the final third, with minimal entries and almost no impact in the opposition's penalty area. Defensively, players like Yerry Mina and Adam Obert showed resilience with clearances and interceptions, but the team struggled to win duels and aerial battles. Goalkeeper Elia Caprile stood out with a strong showing, potentially keeping the scoreline from worsening.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Inter
halftimeSections.text: Inter showcased their superiority throughout the first half, controlling the ball and dictating the tempo of the game. Their attacking intent was clear with multiple shots on target and big chances created, though they missed one significant opportunity. Lautaro Martínez was clinical with his goal, while Alessandro Bastoni played a key role in build-up play, assisting the opener. Defensively, Inter held firm, winning a majority of duels and aerial challenges, with players like Hakan Çalhanoğlu and Stefan de Vrij contributing to a solid backline.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half was overwhelmingly in Inter's favor, with sustained attacking spells from the early minutes that led to the opening goal. They maintained pressure with consistent forays into Cagliari's half, showing intent to add to their tally. Cagliari had brief moments of pushback, particularly towards the middle and end of the half, but these spells were short-lived and lacked the necessary sharpness to shift the game's flow. Inter's attacking waves were relentless, often pinning Cagliari back into their own territory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: For Cagliari, the second half must focus on increasing their attacking presence and converting final third entries into tangible chances. Their midfield needs to assert more control to disrupt Inter's rhythm and create space for forwards like Andrea Belotti. Defensively, they must remain compact to avoid further concessions. Inter, on the other hand, should look to capitalize on their missed chances from the first half and maintain their high press. Avoiding complacency will be crucial to secure their lead and potentially extend it.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Inter continue their dominance if Cagliari cannot find a way to disrupt their flow. Inter's attacking depth and confidence suggest they are poised to add to their lead, especially with players like Martínez and Marcus Thuram pushing forward. Cagliari will need a tactical adjustment, possibly through substitutions or a change in formation, to mount a comeback. However, their lack of threat in the first half makes an equalizer seem challenging unless they can capitalize on a defensive lapse from Inter.
